role_title: Security Product Lead – Product & AI Security

level_signals: 7+ years of experience in cybersecurity, risk management, or technology strategy roles
Owns multi-year roadmap and portfolio-level strategy
Influences without direct authority across engineering, legal, and product stakeholders
M&A security due diligence ownership signals senior scope

must_haves.raw_text_from_jd: Bachelor's degree in Computer Science, Cybersecurity, or related discipline.
7+ years of experience in cybersecurity, risk management, or technology strategy roles.
Demonstrated experience in Product Security (AppSec), DevSecOps, or AI/ML Security domains.
Demonstrated experience building and managing strategic roadmaps tied to measurable outcomes.
Experience providing strategic security due diligence and executing cyber security integration for M&A activities.
Strong understanding of secure development practices, vulnerability management, and common software security frameworks.
Understanding of AI/ML concepts and associated security risks, including data provenance, model integrity, and adversarial machine learning.
Strong product mindset with ability to translate strategy into execution.
Experience working in matrixed organizations with cross-functional stakeholders.
Strong analytical, communication, and executive presentation skills.

explicitly_excluded: Remote work from Hawaii or Alaska

scope.surface: Product Security and AI Security capabilities as internal security products; spans SDLC, DevSecOps, AI/ML risk, and M&A security
primary_metrics_inferred: Capability maturity level (DevSecOps integration, AI risk mitigation completeness)
KPIs tied to roadmap delivery and risk reduction outcomes
core_responsibilities: Develop and maintain a multi-year strategy and roadmap for Product Security and AI Security capabilities
Align roadmap priorities with enterprise risk objectives and regulatory requirements
Lead Security Due Diligence for M&A targets including technical architecture reviews and risk quantification
Define and continuously improve SoFi's M&A security playbook and standards
Define value proposition and service model for Product Security and AI Security capabilities
Maintain and prioritize a strategic backlog aligned to measurable risk reduction outcomes
Mature Secure SDLC practices and embed automation into CI/CD pipelines
Own the portfolio view of Product Security & AI Security initiatives
Monitor industry trends in software supply chain attacks, OWASP Top 10, and AI-specific threats (model poisoning, prompt injection)
Incorporate lessons learned from penetration tests, bug bounty programs, and security audits into roadmap evolution

green_flags: Role has a clearly defined scope spanning two high-priority security domains (Product Security and AI Security)
M&A security ownership is a differentiating and high-visibility responsibility
Position within Security Strategy & Delivery team signals senior strategic mandate
Clear articulation of KPIs, roadmap ownership, and measurable outcomes
Company is a national bank at scale — meaningful product security impact
red_flags: Compensation range not disclosed in the JD body despite being referenced ('The base pay range for this role is listed below') — range appears missing from the posting
Location type is unclear — no explicit remote/hybrid/onsite policy stated beyond exclusion of Hawaii and Alaska
M&A security due diligence is listed as a required qualification but it's a niche background that may be difficult to satisfy
No nice-to-haves listed — all qualifications are framed as requirements, leaving no gradient for candidate self-assessment

fields_inferred_not_stated: seniority.level (inferred from 7+ years requirement, portfolio ownership, and strategic mandate — not explicitly labeled)
seniority.manages_people (inferred as false; role describes influencing without authority, not direct management)
scope.business_model (inferred from company description as B2C fintech / national bank)
scope.primary_metrics_inferred (inferred from responsibilities; not stated as explicit metrics in JD)

location.remote_us_eligible (inferred as true from Hawaii/Alaska exclusion language, implying other remote US locations are permitted)

Location type (remote/hybrid/onsite) is never explicitly stated; the only location signal is exclusion of Hawaii and Alaska for remote work, suggesting some remote eligibility exists but no confirmation of hybrid vs. fully remote vs. onsite
